title: OPENCLAUDIA_ALLOW_OUT_OF_ROOT env var allows model to escape jail via prompt injection on env
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Security and Access / Least Privilege

## Location
src/tools/file/mod.rs:95-97 (strict_mode function)

## Violation
```rust
fn strict_mode() -> bool {
    !matches!(std::env::var("OPENCLAUDIA_ALLOW_OUT_OF_ROOT"), Ok(ref v) if v == "1")
}
```

The jail can be entirely disabled by setting one env var. There is NO logging when the jail is disabled — strict_mode() is called per-resolve_path silently. An operator who set the var "just for one test" and forgot leaves the agent with no path containment. The function is called by all read/write/edit operations.

Worse: the bash tool (separate from this review scope) can `export OPENCLAUDIA_ALLOW_OUT_OF_ROOT=1`. The model itself can then disable its own jail by running a shell command. The jail is theoretical, not enforced.

The CLI flag check is also misnamed: "ALLOW_OUT_OF_ROOT=1" is the only value that disables; any other value (including "true", "yes", "on") leaves strict mode on. The check `if v == "1"` is right-shaped but undocumented at the use site.

## Mandated refactor
1. Cache the env var value at startup in PROJECT_ROOT's LazyLock (once), not per-call. Subsequent bash exports cannot disable the jail.
2. Emit a tracing::warn! once at startup if the jail is disabled.
3. Refuse to honor the env var when the process is launched from a non-interactive context (e.g. when ENV "OPENCLAUDIA_AGENT_MODE=1" or similar), so prompt-injection cannot weaponize an operator's debug setting.
4. Accept the documented set of truthy values ("1", "true", "yes", case-insensitive) — current check is brittle.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
